seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The keyword "deadline" must appear at least twice.
new_constraint: Avoid using the keyword "deadline" in the email.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The keyword "deadline" must appear at least twice. Avoid using the keyword "deadline" in the email.
response:
Subject: Request for Extension - [Project Name] - [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I am writing to respectfully request an extension on the [Project Name] assignment, which is currently due in two days, on [Date].

I have been working diligently on the project, but I've encountered [Briefly and professionally state the reason for the delay - e.g., an unexpected family commitment, a heavier than anticipated workload this week, a technical issue with the data collection]. This has unfortunately impacted the time I have been able to dedicate to bringing the project to the standard I feel it deserves.

I am very committed to submitting high-quality work for this course, and I believe a short extension would allow me to finalize the remaining sections properly. Would it be possible to have an extension until [Suggest a new, realistic date]?

I understand the importance of adhering to established submission dates, and I apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause regarding the current submission time. I am eager to meet the requirements, and a little more time would greatly help me meet the intended submission criteria for this assignment.

Thank you very much for considering my situation. I look forward to your reply.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number]<turn|>
